Hello friends and welcome to the fourth installment of Six Figure Emails. This one is called The Spoiler Alert aka The Fortune Teller. So we're gonna talk about some fun stuff today. We're gonna be talking about marketers, fortune tellers, and why I told all the other kids at school in grade two that Wayne Gretzky was my dad. True story, but it never happened. So we're gonna get into all that in just a sec. This is how it is going to go down. First we're gonna start off with a little bit of a background on the template, how it came to be, why we use it, what are the persuasion principles at play, what's actually going on that makes this effective, where to use it within a long-term never-going sequence, and then of course how to use it including some powerful questions to ask to extract the content for the email, the template itself, and then we're going to take a look at a few examples in the videos below this one. Cool, let's dive in. So the background, I struggled quite a bit with future pacing early on in my copyrighting career. It's this big, talked-about topic, essentially the short version of it is in pacing out the future actions or the future that our prospect will experience with our product. We kind of write the script in their mind of what actions they need to take next and we also create safety around it, right? Because it's no longer this great mystery but something they can envision. They're already kind of embodying the experience of owning your product or going through the conversion process in their minds and that creates a certain pattern that makes it a little bit easier for them to actually do in real life. So that's the short version of it and while I knew this I just kind of struggled with it. I would write the typical things like all you have to do is go on the next page, click that big red button, you'll get to the checkout page, you'll enter your details and you'll enter our membership area, something like that. But it just felt like it lacked some extra conversion power. It didn't feel like it really added to the process. It wasn't bad by any stretch but it just didn't feel like it was worth the space it was taking up on the page. So that's what got me thinking, why am I doing this wrong or just not as effective as it could be? And in that question I turned to two top marketers. I turned to Miss Cleo. Some of you might remember her from your childhood or earlier years who is this famous on-screen TV Colin Psychic who is absolutely everywhere and she turned out to be a total quack I think. I don't think she had any clairvoyance or any kind of insight into anybody's future but for some reason thousands upon thousands of people were willing to pay her money for a glimpse into their future including my mother who is a single mom at the time. We were about eight or nine years old and she wanted to inquire a little bit about her future life partner and Miss Cleo being Miss Cleo described essentially Wayne Gretzky to a T saying that he was a famous hockey player of that age already has kids and is breaking NHL records as we speak. So she essentially told me that my father would be Wayne Gretzky. I believed her. I spread that news far and wide and unfortunately couldn't back it up. So Miss Cleo despite her lack of clairvoyance made millions of dollars predicting the future and how is that even possible? It's possible because learning about our future is super addictive and compelling even if we don't completely believe it it's super compelling and the other person who has really made a living off of future pacing or at least use that with as an element within his greater spectrum of work is Tony Robbins. Future pacing is a critical element within NLP and coaching and when we talk about coaching the conversion which we're doing with these email templates using a tool like Future Pacing in the way that someone like Tony Robbins or anyone in the coaching world would to encourage behavioral change or shifts on a deeper level that's something we're very curious about doing in our work as copywriters. So all of this got me thinking as a whole people are addicted to hearing about their future even if they don't initially or fully trust the source like the multi-billion dollar psychic industry. NLP and coaching uses this mechanism not as a sales technique per se but they kind of are they are selling you on new behaviors or outcomes in your life. They're selling you on new paradigms you can embody and in our case we're going to be using it to coach the precursors to the conversion coach our prospects and readers into who they have to be in order to say yes to our product so in that sense we are doing exactly that and if we can properly leverage both people's addiction to learning about their future and also use it as a mechanism for behavioral shifts and a shift in views like the coaching world does then all of a sudden we have a very powerful mechanism to coach the conversion but most importantly so that's what works but what was behind the world's most epic future pace fail so this is the face of an unsuccessful marketer many people will remember the so-called epic monologue he gave on the phone here it's become kind of one of the more famous scenes in all film over the last 20 years but it failed because the movie continued and his daughter still got kidnapped so this speech failed but let's read it quickly and see why this was an unsuccessful future pace so Liam Neeson in Taken says I don't know who you are I don't know what you want if you're looking for ransom I can tell you I don't have money what I do have are a very particular set of skills that's not very specific skills I have acquired over a very long career skills that make me a nightmare well once again nightmare is a very general term for people like you if you let my daughter go now that'll be the end of it I will not look for you I will not pursue you but if you don't I will look for you I will find you and I will kill you so it makes for compelling film it's a great monologue from a writing perspective but an awful sales script that did not convert the captors of his daughter and here's why so the first thing is it's lacking specificity you know we have to assume as copywriters as marketers that our readers imagination muscle has totally atrophied in the digital age we can't expect them to work to envision what we are trying to say and words like a particular set of skills I don't know what you want in nightmare these are just vague placeholders for the actual experience of what a nightmare is or what those skills are or entail so that's the first mistake Liam Neeson made which you know gave us great movie to watch after second thing is they're not grounded or anchored in a present reality you know the stronger you anchor your picture on the safe shores of their current and familiar reality the further into the horizon you could pull them where a lot of future pacing fails is they're only talking about the future but it feels so far and disconnected from where they are right now so you really have to start off your future pace by describing exactly what is going on right now and then you are in the right to stretch that more into the horizon last thing not assertive or authoritative enough now Liam Neeson was plenty authoritative there but as marketers we tend to not be assertive or authoritative we tend to speculate but we don't want to do that we actually want to dictate this is where trust come from so the famous marketing words imagine if or imagine that that's a good start but that could feel more like a temporary dreamlike escape instead of a concrete foregone conclusion and that's what you want to offer them you want to confidently and unapologetically pace out their reality for them as you see it happening and this is what builds trust and authority as you do so so that's those are the three biggest future pacing blunders both in Liam Neeson's world and in the copyrighting world and with that said let's talk about more why we use future pacing especially the version that we're going to be diving into in this template so first thing we want to look at a lot of our marketing appropriately focuses on the prospects pace prospects past sorry we talk about their past failures the pain they've been going through all that tends to be in the past whereas our prospects focus especially during a launcher promotion isn't actually in the past or in the future it's actually centered around the anxiety of the present buying decision especially in the closing phases of a launch and rarely do we have the opportunity to harness both the power of future pacing also in a focused directed and highly concentrated way that is anchored in the present so stay with me here we are kind of bending time but by not using future pacing in a directed way we actually rob our prospect of the joy in projecting themselves into a new version of themselves and growing into it so they are anxious about the present we're talking about their past and rarely do we both come together and look at what the immediate future and long-term future of us working together might look like and that is something that we are totally robbing them of and it's costing us sales so when you as well future pacing will elucidate and ramp up desire for the outcomes your offer will create because it becomes palpable it becomes part of their experienced imagined reality and if they could experience it experience it in their imagination that is where desire actually comes from it also makes the promise feel more believable since it's anchored and rooted in the present context this is why we start off by talking about the present if we could highlight that in a way that is accurate and builds trust then all of a sudden we are also the authority to predict the future not miss Cleo but someone actually knowledgeable about what will happen if they continue to have that path with us it also helps dissolve resistance and objections around the purchase because we're forcing them we're walking them through that within our future pace so it becomes less of this hidden shadow robbing them of the potential to buy and more something that is clear we're actually future pacing how they overcame that resistance and objection and finally it makes that purchase decision feel safe and natural because once again it's part of an imagined reality that we've already walked them through so as a whole what is this template coaching while we're coaching the experience of owning your product including the desire it fulfills the problems it overcomes the new opportunities it opens them up to all these things is included in our future pace we're also coaching the joyful experience of overcoming their present decision anxiety around buying that this is an important one that most future pacing leaves out give them the joyful experience of overcoming their present anxiety we're also coaching normalization of those resistances we're not shaming it and saying your tire kicker or anything like that we're saying it's totally normal but here is how we can get through it together here's how you could actually be consistent with someone who would buy and get these results we're coaching belief and trust in the possibility of achieving the outcomes we're also distancing from the unfavorable reality of not owning our product and we'll show what that looks like in the second example which is where we could actually contrast realities of having our product and not having it and what that looks like 12 hours from now 24 hours from now two weeks from now two months from now we could actually contrast realities in our future pace and of course risk reversal in more of an advanced way but in future pacing what it looks like with our product and what it looks like when they buy we give them a chance to digest and metabolize the future before it happens before it happens and that will tame the wizard brain and help our prospect overcome the fear of uncertainty alright so all that said where to use it this is traditionally used as a final day email but have recently experiment experimented with sending it the evening before the last day so our prospect just goes to bed with those ideas future-paced in their head and that's also a place where we tend to kind of metabolize our day right in our dream state so sometimes it could be cool to try sending it there but obviously you can't bank on someone actually reading it before bed so last day if you're feeling adventurous you could send it the evening before how to use it there are a few questions to ask this is what you want a journal on before writing this email these are this is really where you're gonna get your content from but what are the very specific thought patterns or layers of resistance they'll have to overcome before hitting purchase so what is that resistance exactly to the best of your ability describe their current reality as accurately as you can this is what we mean by anchoring your future pace into the present and where we're going to be starting the email so what is going on in their current reality you want to be as accurate and specific as possible and then this is where we extend it what will their experience look like 12 to 72 hours after buying your product what would it look like if they didn't buy your product and then what will it look like a few weeks or a few years or a few months after buying your product and then a few weeks months or years after buying your product and you can use those if you're gonna contrast it cool seven things you can future pace you can future pace the immediate next action steps so they're gonna click on this link they're gonna watch a certain video they're going to enter their details on the checkout page whatever it looks like the immediate next steps you can feature pace that you can feature pace the next few days what exactly does that look like when they're engaging with your product are they unboxing something are they logging into a site and watching videos and downloading workbooks or whatever it looks like just future pace the next few days you can feature pace new world views understandings perspectives and perceptions that will emerge in interacting with your product accomplishments achievements and outcomes that they'll enjoy through your product reactions or reactions to their new circumstances that contrast to their present reactions so what are the new circumstances that emerge and using your product and what is their reaction to that how others will relate to them in a new desirable way based off of what your product delivers and then of course future decisions you know every decision we make creates a new array and new spectrum of choices that we have in front of us so what are those new decisions and options they have as a result of taking the option of buying your product so those are a few things you could play with really it's endless you could future pace pretty much anything but these are just a few starting points as they most closely relate to the product cool so the template itself remember we start off by being hyper specific and anchor it in their current reality you know this is what you are currently going through and as accurately as you can paint that because that's what's going to earn you the trust to tether it out into the future and then an immediate future pace of ten minutes from now what would they be doing so this is really about disarming fear and resistance to the purchase and short-term future pace of 24 hours or whatever the immediate experience of owning the product is so this could help disarm potential buyers remorse and then you're going to have a call to action and then the medium term future pace so two to three days quick wins as it relates to a unique aspect of your offer so what is the what is the quickest most exciting or most valuable win they would enjoy through your product that's only you could future pace over here then the next call to action and then a long-term future pace and now we're extending it even further out to two to three weeks and maybe a whole paradigm or understanding of the industry or the world has shifted in profound ways reality has flipped what outcomes have started to become realized through those realizations in working with your product then another call to action and then a long-term future pace for eight weeks or even further into the distance tangible ongoing and growing results that emerge from that new world so what what decisions are now on their table what maybe new problems have arisen due to their initial batch of successes anything that would fit into this window you can include here another call to action and then finally the long-term future pace 10 plus weeks what it's like experiencing that new ultimate outcome that your product delivers and the experiences it now forwards them any new decisions that they have to make you want to include that so that is what it looks like as a whole and in the next video below this one we will start to break down a few a few templates so that we can see how it looks like in action I'll see in the next video
